statement.en: Local Elite Competition and Conspiracy in Regional Expansion in the Indonesian Archipelago Region
statement.summary: This study aims to analyze and explain the forms of competition and conspiracy of local elites in regional expansion in the Indonesian archipelago. The research approach uses qualitative descriptive, with data collection through observation, interviews and documentation as well as data analysis through data collection, data assessment, data interpretation, data analysis, and data conclusions. To obtain data validity, this analysis will use data triangulation techniques, which means conducting cross-checks between one data source and another, and between sources. The findings of this study concluded that competition among local elites in the expansion was due to each elite, both on Obi Island, Bacan City, and Ternate City, having political interests after the regional expansion. The priorit

proposer: Bakri La Suhu, Abdul Halil Hi Ibrahim, Darwin Abd Radjak

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper describes empirical social science findings (local elite competition patterns) that are provisional, context-dependent, and undergoing validation. Qualitative research without formal theorem structure or peer-reviewed publication status indicates FLOWING classification.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 地域拡大における権力の重なりが、協力と競争の比率を決める神秘的な釣り合いがある。
